---
title: "index.qmd"
format: html
editor: visual
---

---

title: "Time Series Analysis of Ventura County Wildfires"
subtitle: "Using SARIMA and ARMAX Models"
author: "Edgar Gonzalez Martinez"
format: html
------------

## Project Overview

This project analyzes monthly wildfire activity in Ventura County, California from 2000 to 2020 using time series methods.

The first part of the project models monthly wildfire starts using a SARIMA model. The second part models monthly acres burned using an ARMAX model with external predictors related to drought, Santa Ana-like wind conditions, weather, and NDVI vegetation greenness.

## Research Questions

1. Can monthly wildfire starts in Ventura County be modeled using SARIMA methods?
2. Can external predictors improve the modeling of monthly acres burned?
3. Which environmental variables are most useful for explaining wildfire severity?

## Data

The project uses monthly wildfire data from Ventura County along with external drought, weather, wind, and vegetation variables.

The main variables are:

* Monthly wildfire count
* Monthly total acres burned
* SPI drought categories
* NASA POWER weather variables
* Santa Ana-like wind indicators
* MODIS NDVI vegetation greenness

## Methods

The analysis uses two main models:

* SARIMA model for monthly wildfire starts
* ARMAX model for monthly acres burned

The SARIMA model provides a baseline time series model using only past wildfire behavior. The ARMAX model adds external predictors to explain wildfire severity while still accounting for time dependence.

## Main Results

The selected SARIMA model for wildfire starts was:

$$
ARIMA(0,1,3)(0,0,1)_{12}
$$

For monthly acres burned, the ARMAX model selected three main predictors:

* Lagged severe drought
* Santa Ana-like wind indicator
* Mean NDVI

The ARMAX model improved AICc and BIC compared with the baseline SARIMA model for acres burned.

## Tools Used

* R
* Quarto
* forecast
* dplyr
* ggplot2
* MASS
* car
* NASA POWER data
* MODIS NDVI data
* SPI drought data

## Links

* [Full Report](report.html)
* [GitHub Repository](https://github.com/CheddyG/Ventura-County-Wildfire-Analysis)
